global.dont_know: I don't know / To be observed

monitoring.intro_text: This tool is not a medical device but an interactive guide. It was designed to help you check if your follow-up is in accordance with the official recommendations of the
monitoring.vigilance_critical: Vigilance: some critical exams (MRI/Kidney/CT) do not seem up to date.
monitoring.interpretation: Evaluation of adherence to medical recommendations (PNDS)
monitoring.warn_eeg: Neurology: Discuss the need for a follow-up EEG based on seizure activity.
monitoring.warn_dermato: Dermatology: A specialized evaluation should be considered in case of pain or discomfort.
monitoring.warn_pneumo_doc: Pulmonology: Pulmonary status insufficiently documented; check medical history.
monitoring.warn_cardio_init: Cardiology: Check the initial cardiac history.
monitoring.q1_1: Do you or your child currently have seizures?
monitoring.q1_3: Has a neuropsychiatric (TAND) screening been performed?
monitoring.q1_5: When was the last brain MRI?
monitoring.q2_1: Has kidney involvement related to TSC already been identified?
monitoring.q2_2: When was the last abdominal MRI or renal ultrasound?
monitoring.q2_3: When was the last blood and urine test (kidney function)?
monitoring.q2_4: When was blood pressure last measured?
monitoring.q3_1: Do you have one or more skin involvements related to TSC?
monitoring.q4_1: Have you ever consulted a pulmonologist for TSC follow-up?
monitoring.q4_2: Do you experience unusual shortness of breath or exertional dyspnea?
monitoring.q4_3: Have pulmonary cysts already been identified?
monitoring.q4_4: When was the last chest CT scan?
monitoring.q4_5: When was the last pulmonary function test (PFT)?
monitoring.q4_6: When was the last 6-minute walk test?
monitoring.q5_1: Have you had fundus lesions or vision disorders?
monitoring.q5_2: When was the last ophthalmological consultation?
monitoring.q6_1: Has a cardiac rhabdomyoma ever been diagnosed in your child?
monitoring.q6_2: Does your child have symptoms or heart failure?
monitoring.q6_3: When was the last cardiac ultrasound?
monitoring.q7_1: Has a dental panoramic X-ray already been performed?
monitoring.q7_2: When was the last dental consultation?
monitoring.disclaimer_text: This information is provided for guidance according to PNDS. Consult your doctor to adapt these exams to your personal situation.
monitoring.conclusion_warning: ⚠️ Vigilance: Follow-up needs to be optimized for this organ.
monitoring.action_q1_2: Discuss the benefit of a follow-up EEG.
monitoring.action_q1_4: Perform an initial baseline TAND screening.
monitoring.action_q1_5: Schedule a follow-up brain MRI (recommended every 1 to 3 years before age 25).
monitoring.action_q2_2: Schedule a follow-up renal imaging (MRI or Ultrasound).
monitoring.action_q2_3: Perform a blood and urine test (creatinine, proteinuria).
monitoring.action_q3_1: Consult a dermatologist to evaluate skin lesions.
monitoring.action_q4_1: Perform a baseline consultation with a pulmonologist.
monitoring.action_q4_2: Investigate shortness of breath through a specialist consultation.
monitoring.action_q4_4: Update chest CT scan (recommended every 2-3 years if cysts present).
monitoring.action_q4_7: Update surveillance chest CT scan (every 5-10 years).
monitoring.action_q7_1: Perform a baseline dental panoramic X-ray (before age 6).

home.welcome_title: Welcome to your TSC Tracking Diary
home.welcome_text: This application supports you daily in monitoring Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (TSC).
home.beta_warning: Evaluation version (Beta) - Data is stored locally on your device.

tand.step_indicator: Section {etape} of {total} - This questionnaire helps screen for TAND.
tand.recovery_msg: Some answers have been recovered from your previous evaluation to save time.
tand.dont_know: I don't know / To be observed
tand.if_yes: If you answered YES to any of these questions:
tand.step1_intro: Let's start by talking about {prenom}'s development to get an idea of their level. How old was {prenom} when {pronom}:
tand.q1_a_text: a. smiled for the first time?
tand.q1_d_text: d. used single words other than mama/dada?
tand.q1_e_text: e. used 2-word phrases or short sentences?
tand.q1_f_text: f. was toilet trained during the day?
tand.q1_g_text: g. was toilet trained at night?
tand.step3_intro: Let's talk about behaviors that might concern you. Has {prenom} ever had any of the following difficulties:
tand.q3_8: Absent or delayed language for communication
tand.q3_9: Repeating words or phrases over and over
tand.q3_11: Difficulty getting along with other people of the same age
tand.q3_12: Repetitive behavior (doing the same thing over and over)
tand.q3_13: Rigidity or inflexibility about how to do things / dislike of changing routine
tand.q3_18: Eating difficulties (eating too much/too little or unusual things)
tand.q3_19: Sleep difficulties (falling asleep or waking up)
tand.q3_help1: Have you had a comprehensive evaluation or help for this difficulty?
tand.q3_help2: Would you like a more comprehensive evaluation or help for this?
tand.step4_intro: Some problematic behaviors can combine to meet criteria for specific psychiatric disorders. Has {prenom} ever received a diagnosis of:
tand.q4_1: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), such as autism or Asperger syndrome
tand.q4_3: Anxiety disorder, panic attacks, phobia, separation anxiety disorder
tand.q4_help1: Have you had a comprehensive evaluation or help for this diagnosis?
tand.q4_help2: Would you like a more comprehensive evaluation or help for this?
tand.step5_info: About half of people with TSC will have significant difficulties in their global intellectual development and may have an intellectual disability.
tand.q5_a: a. Have you ever felt concerned about this for {prenom}?
tand.q5_b: b. Has {prenom} ever had a formal intelligence evaluation by a professional using IQ tests?
tand.q5_b_res: If YES, what were the results:
tand.q5_c: c. What is your opinion on {prenom}'s intellectual ability / your intellectual ability?
tand.q5_d: d. Would you like a further evaluation or help for this?
tand.step6_info: Many school-aged people with TSC have difficulties at school.
tand.step6_intro: Does {prenom} have or has {pronom} had difficulties with the following:
tand.q6_help1: Has {prenom} had a comprehensive evaluation or help for this?
tand.q6_help2: Has {prenom} been offered additional support at school?
tand.q6_help3: Would you like a further evaluation or help for {prenom}?
tand.step7_intro: Most people with TSC will have difficulties with specific cognitive skills. Does {prenom} have difficulties with:
tand.q7_2: b. Attention (concentrating well, not getting distracted)
tand.q7_3: c. Dual-tasking / multi-tasking (doing two things at once)
tand.q7_4: d. Visuo-spatial tasks (doing a puzzle or using building blocks)
tand.q7_5: e. Executive skills (planning, organizing, flexible thinking)
tand.q7_6: f. Orientation skills (forgetting the date or where you are)
tand.q7_help1: Has {prenom} had a comprehensive evaluation or help for this?
tand.q7_help2: Would you like a further evaluation or help for these difficulties?
tand.step8_intro: Besides the difficulties mentioned above, TSC can have a significant impact on people's lives for other reasons. Does {prenom} have one or more of the following difficulties:
tand.q8_2: b. Very high level of stress in the family (e.g., between siblings)
tand.q8_3: c. Very high level of stress between parents, leading to significant relationship difficulties
tand.q8_help1: Has {prenom} and/or your family had an evaluation or help for this?
tand.q8_help2: Would you like a further evaluation or help for this?
tand.step9_intro: Of all the difficulties discussed above, how much have they disrupted, troubled, or upset you/your children/your family? (0: Not at all, 10: Extremely)
tand.step10_intro: Of all the concerns discussed above, what are your main priorities that you would like to work on next?
tand.step11_intro: Do you have any other concerns about TSC-Associated Neuropsychiatric Disorders that we haven't discussed in this checklist?
tand.q11_text: Do you have any other unaddressed concerns?
tand.step12_intro: Interviewer rating of the impact/burden on the individual/child/family? (0: Not at all, 10: Extremely)
tand.q12_slider: Impact / burden on the family (0 to 10)

treatments.title: Treatment Plan
treatments.dialog_hint: e.g. 1 pill in the morning, 1 in the evening...

understand_tsc.title: Understanding TSC
understand_tsc.sec1_text:
Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (TSC) is a rare genetic disease. It is part of the so-called neurocutaneous diseases, which can influence the development of certain tissues in the body. It is linked to a modification of genes that normally play an important role in controlling cell growth and organization.

In people with TSC, this control mechanism works less well. Certain cells can then multiply or organize in an unusual way, leading to the formation of small benign lesions called hamartomas. These lesions are not cancers: they are tissues made up of normal cells, but which have developed in a disorganized manner.

Today, knowledge about TSC has progressed significantly. The biological mechanisms involved are better understood, in particular the role of a cellular signaling pathway called mTOR, which regulates cell growth.
understand_tsc.sec2_text:
The disease is caused by a modification of one of the two genes called TSC1 or TSC2. In about two-thirds of cases, this modification appears spontaneously, without any family history.

In other situations, the disease can be passed from parent to child in a mode called autosomal dominant transmission. This means that a person carrying the mutation can pass the disease to their child.
understand_tsc.sec3_text:
An important feature of Tuberous Sclerosis Complex is its great variability of expression. Two people with the disease, even within the same family, can have very different manifestations.

Some people show few signs and live with little impact on their daily lives, while others may require more regular medical follow-up.
understand_tsc.sec4_text:
These advances have allowed the development of targeted treatments and adapted follow-up approaches.

Management is generally based on regular and multidisciplinary medical follow-up, in order to best support each person according to their needs. Patient associations also play an essential role by providing information, support, and sharing of experiences to the families concerned.

Even if TSC is a chronic disease, progress in research and management makes it possible today to considerably improve the quality of life and prospects of the people concerned.

care_plan.title: My Medical Follow-up
care_plan.algorithm_desc: This algorithm is based on international medical recommendations (Consensus Guidelines) to indicate the exams to schedule.
care_plan.check_updated: Check if your follow-up is up to date
care_plan.neuro_q1: Have you had any epileptic seizures recently?
care_plan.neuro_q1_orange: During an appointment with your neurologist, ask if an EEG is recommended.
care_plan.neuro_q2: When was your last brain MRI?
care_plan.neuro_q2_red_young: An MRI is recommended every 1 to 3 years until age 25.
care_plan.neuro_q2_orange_adult: In adulthood, MRIs are less frequent. Ask your doctor for advice.
care_plan.neuro_q2_orange_young: Check if a follow-up MRI is scheduled soon.
care_plan.neuro_q2_green: Brain imaging is up to date.
care_plan.nephro_q1: When was your last renal imaging (MRI/CT/Ultrasound)?
care_plan.nephro_q1_red: Abdominal imaging every 1 to 3 years is highly recommended.
care_plan.nephro_q1_orange: Ask your doctor if a renal ultrasound or MRI is planned this year.
care_plan.nephro_q1_green: Renal morphological follow-up is up to date.
care_plan.nephro_q2: When was your last blood test (renal function / GFR)?
care_plan.nephro_q2_orange: An evaluation of renal function and blood pressure is recommended once a year.
care_plan.nephro_q2_green: Biological check-up is up to date.
care_plan.dermato_q1: Do you have any skin manifestations (angiofibromas, etc.)?
care_plan.dermato_q1_orange: An annual consultation with a specialized dermatologist is recommended.
care_plan.dermato_q1_green: Simple monitoring. Report any new lesions.
care_plan.pneumo_q1: Do you have exertional dyspnea, unexplained shortness of breath, or chest pain?
care_plan.pneumo_q1_red: Talk to your doctor promptly to screen for potential lung involvement (LAM).
care_plan.pneumo_q1_green: To be monitored. Report any abnormal shortness of breath.
care_plan.pneumo_q2: Have you ever had a high-resolution chest CT scan?
care_plan.pneumo_q2_red: A baseline scan is recommended in adult women (often around age 18 or at adult diagnosis).
care_plan.pneumo_q2_orange: Ask if a new scan is necessary based on your symptoms.
care_plan.pneumo_q2_green: Lung screening is up to date.
care_plan.dentist_q1: When was your last dental check-up?
care_plan.dentist_q1_orange: A check-up every 6 months is recommended to monitor enamel and gums.
care_plan.dentist_q1_green: Regular dental follow-up is up to date.
care_plan.dentist_q2: Has a dental panoramic X-ray been done recently?
care_plan.dentist_q2_orange: Check with your dentist, a panoramic X-ray is recommended to check dental eruption.

report.title: TAND Medical Report
report.subtitle: Summary generated for medical consultation
report.no_issues: No major issues reported during screening.
